body.title: Bootstrap inference graph from all historical chat logs
body.result: bootstrap_learning.py: chunked session processor with configurable N, pre-chunk scope logging, human abort prompt, flat-file idempotency; 21 tests
body.description:
Run all existing chat logs (CC session transcripts + Igor conversation history) through the inference learning pipeline to bootstrap the knowledge graph before the live system has accumulated enough calls to learn from. Chunked processing with cost monitoring — each chunk is a metered unit, not a fire-and-forget bulk job.

Goal: the graph tree starts populated from years of existing patterns rather than learning from scratch on the first live inference call.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/export_chat.py or new bootstrap_runner.py; devices/librarian/learning_pipeline.py (reuse)

**Design rules:** no-sqlite; cost-monitored chunked processing (never unbounded batch); log every chunk with token count and cost

**Scope boundary:** IN — chunked processing of existing transcripts; cost monitoring per chunk; progress tracking. OUT — live inference pipeline (T-inference-learning-pipeline); real-time processing

**Completion criteria:** (1) all transcripts processable in N-chunk batches where N is configurable; (2) cost per chunk logged before next chunk fires; (3) human can abort between chunks; (4) idempotent — re-running doesn't duplicate graph nodes
